libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Before creating CacheManager/cache instances, disable Ehcache's update check with System.setProperty("net.sf.ehcache.skipUpdateCheck", "true") or equivalent config. For transaction-manager tests, set bitronix.tm.configuration to a temporary properties file with a unique serverId, disableJmx=true, and journal=null; shut down the transaction manager and clear the property after each test. Use temporary diskStore paths and shut down CacheManager instances to avoid cross-test state.

deterministic_setup.reason: ehcache-core declares JTA as provided and imports javax.transaction.* for XA/local transaction support; JDK 25 only supplies javax.transaction.xa, so tests covering transaction modes need this API on the test classpath.

DefaultTransactionManagerLookup discovers Bitronix as its built-in fallback transaction manager, and exercising XA registration/lookup meaningfully requires a concrete transaction manager implementation.
Bitronix references JMS APIs through optional classes; adding the JMS spec avoids classpath/native-image analysis failures when Bitronix classes are loaded or analyzed.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8877 [Automation] native-image run fails for net.sf.ehcache:ehcache-core:2.5.0; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Hibernate, servlet, and Terracotta integration classes have additional provided/external dependencies; avoid those paths unless intentionally adding more setup.
Terracotta clustered-cache coverage would require an external server, but embedded Ehcache core coverage is reachable without Docker.
Bitronix uses JVM-global transaction-manager state, so tests must isolate configuration and cleanup carefully.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Ehcache 2.x core is embedded and needs no Docker service, but meaningful coverage of its transaction paths requires non-transitive/provided JTA APIs plus a concrete transaction manager setup.
